raw_consumer_statement: In XXXX, my Sears Citibank Sears Mastercard had several fraudulent charges posted. I was alerted to such charges by Citibank and I contacted them immediately. It took over a week for the charges to finally get taken off. I believe the charges totaled less than {$1000.00}. I thought the issue was resolved and I also requested that the account be closed permanently because this was like the XXXX or XXXX time in about a year that this fraudulent situation had occurred. And, this was not an easy decision because I held that account for well over 25 years. Then in the last week of XXXX, XXXX I had received a letter from Citibank Retail Services ( dated XX/XX/XXXX ) indicating that they were pushing XXXX of the charges back onto my account because they did not have enough support to resolved the dispute. So, now I sit with an approximate balance of about {$260.00} that they expect me to pay. I called them on XX/XX/XXXX and I was told that I had to write them a letter explaining ( again ) why these charges were not legitimate and mail it to them. I find this whole thing so concerning and absurd. At this point, I may have to make make a payment on the account to ensure they do not report me to credit agencies ( and I have excellent credit - so that can not happen ). I am asking for immediate resolution and response from Citibank Sears Mastercard. In the meantime, I will keep trying to reach someone within the US ( Citibank seems to send all their customer call offshore from the US ).
